content.problemStatement:
Andrash study in Uzhland National University. Now is the time of exam results. Andrash similar to other students, hopes that this scores in the exam could fetch him a scholarship/stipendium for his studies.

There are following simple rules to receive stipendium:



University follows **5** point grading system. In an exam, a student can receive any score from 2 to 5. 2 is called an F grade, meaning that student has failed that exam.
Student should not have fail any of the exams.
Student must obtain a full score in some of his/her exams to show that he/she is excellent in some of the subjects.
He/She must have a grade point average not less than **4.0**


You are given information regarding the exams and how Andrash performed in those. Can you help him figure out whether he will receive the stipendium or not!!
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains an integer N, the number of examinations. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting Andrash's score for each exam.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'Yes' if Andrash will receive the stipendium, or 'No' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, the average grade point score is 3.8 which is less than 4.0, so Andrash will not receive stipendium. In the second test case, Andrash satisfies all the criteria for getting stipend.
Andrash has full scores in all exams and his average grade point score is 5.0 which is greater than or equal to 4.0, so he will receive stipend.
Andrash has failed one exam, so he will not receive stipend.

solution.approach: To determine if Andrash will receive the stipendium, we need to check three conditions: (1) Andrash must not have failed any exam, (2) Andrash must have obtained a full score in at least one exam, and (3) Andrash's grade point average must be at least 4.0. We calculate the average by summing all scores and dividing by the number of exams. If all conditions are met, Andrash will receive the stipendium.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N), where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of examinations in a test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1), as we only use a constant amount of space to store the sum of scores, the count of full scores, and other variables.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    scores = list(map(int, input().split()))
    sum_scores = sum(scores)
    count = scores.count(5)
    if any(score < 3 for score in scores) or count == 0 or sum_scores / N < 4.0:
        print('No')
    else:
        print('Yes')
